result:
Review written to `docs/implementation/structured-export-plugin/review.md`.

**Headline findings:**

- **B1 — `preset` never round-trips.** `Prefs.load()` (Prefs.lua:22-33) doesn't read the `preset` key that `ExportDialog.save` writes, so the last-used preset always falls back to `'print'`. Violates spec line 33. The Batch D log explicitly hand-waved this as "fine" — it isn't.
- **B2 — Type-check mismatch.** `ExportTask.filterSelection` admits `LrPublishedCollectionSet`, but `Collections.enumerate` only branches on the literal string `'LrCollectionSet'`. A Published Collection Set falls into the bare-collection branch and will crash on `:getPhotos()`.

**Non-blocking highlights:** Content Credentials pcall fallback is dead code (plain table assignment can't fail, so the legacy key is never set); exiftool probe leaks a `/dev/null` handle; `LrDialogs.message(...)` uses single-arg form, rendering the full sentence as the title with no body; prefs_spec has only 2 tests and no preset coverage (would have caught B1). Full list in the review file.

**Verify:** `busted` → 88 specs pass; `luacheck` → 15 files, 0 warnings.
